name: Butternut Squash with Onions and Sage
recipeIngredient: 4 tablespoons Olive or vegetable oil, lite
8 cups Butternut squash, cut into 1/2-inch cubes
2 tablespoons Sage leaves, chopped, OR 2 teaspoons Sage, dried
recipeInstructions:
Preheat oven to 350 degrees. Lightly oil or butter a gratin dish.
Warm half the oil in a skillet, add onions and thyme and cook over medium-high heat. Stir frequently, until onions are lightly caramelized, about 12 to 15 minutes. Add half the garlic, season with salt and pepper and cook for 3 more minutes.
Dredge squash in flour. Heat remaining oil in a large pan, add squash and saute until it begins to brown, about 7 minutes. Add sage and parsley, season with salt and plenty of pepper; cook another minute or so.
Spread onion mixture on bottom of dish; add squash and cheese. Pour in milk and cover with bread crumbs. Cover and bake for 25 minutes; remove cover and bake for another 25 minutes, until top is browned. Serves 4.
Per serving: 479 cal; 16 g prot; 16 g fat; 67 g carb; 32 mg chol; 447 mg sod; 15 g fiber; lacto Vegetarian Times, Nov 93/MM by DEEANNE
